element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core defensive structure; regenerates mana, stores gold, produces Imps, detects threats
Primary workforce for digging, claiming tiles, fortifying walls
Economic foundation; gems provide unlimited supply for late-game scaling

element_introduction_order.element: Primary rooms (Lair, Hatchery, Training Room, Library, Treasury)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Infrastructure unlocks creature recruitment, feeding, training, research, and resource management
Creature type attracted depends on dungeon composition; unlocks combat capabilities
Each has unique abilities, expertise, and job preferences for tactical composition
Basic defensive structures; block enemies while allowing friendly passage
Passive defense system; distributed placement creates layered deterrent
Caps at experience level 4; higher levels require Combat Pit or real combat
Creature-versus-creature arena for experience farming beyond level 4
Secret doors provide concealment; Magic doors provide active defense
Casino exploits creatures for gold; Workshop produces defensive infrastructure; Torture converts enemies
Enable dungeon expansion across hazard terrain; Stone immune to lava
Environmental damage source; requires bridge infrastructure to cross safely
Obtained via corpse conversion; cannot be attracted through normal Portal recruitment
Increases work output but damages creature health; risk-reward micromanagement
End-goal of each level; recover all 20 gems to open surface portal

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Multi-phase progression: early economic buildup, mid-game infrastructure expansion, late-game creature composition optimization toward Portal Gem acquisition. Campaign spans 20 levels with escalating complexity.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Multiple level versions with differing attack methods allow player choice in approach strategy
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Secret doors imitate walls, providing concealment for defensive positioning

difficulty_curve.early_game: Player starts with Dungeon Heart and Imps; focus on basic infrastructure (Lair, Hatchery, Treasury) and gold mining. Simple creature recruitment and defense against initial hero incursions.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Introduction of advanced rooms (Combat Pit, Casino, Torture Chamber) and upgraded defensive structures (Magic doors, Secret doors). Multi-creature composition strategy; creature experience management becomes critical.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Portal Gem acquisition under heavy enemy pressure. Undead creature recruitment, high-level creature teams (level 9-10), sophisticated trap networks, and resource optimization against multiple enemy threats.
notable_spikes: First hero invasion: tests early defensive capability
Combat Pit unlocking: forces tactical creature composition decisions
Enemy Keeper emergence: shifts from PvE to PvP defense
Portal Gem access attempts: high-stakes final objectives

notable_level_patterns.description: Early levels establish gold/gem mining, basic room infrastructure, and creature recruitment pipeline. Player learns Imp management and Portal mechanics.
Mid-levels unlock advanced rooms and defensive structures. Dungeon footprint grows; environmental hazards (lava, water) introduce bridge crossing requirements.
Mid-to-late levels emphasize Combat Pit training, creature leveling to caps (4, then via combat to 10), and synergistic team building for Portal Gem defense.
Late levels present simultaneous hero invasions and enemy Keeper attacks. Player balances offense (Portal Gem acquisition), defense (trap networks), and creature management.
